---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: a032bcb2bd50824b90ac9f363946e058724fb9f9
findings:
  - { id: judged-preflight-detail-skips-authoritative-source-filter, kind: judged, text: "unmetACDetail (cmd/verdi/closepreflight.go:209,228) computes per-kind satisfaction over the raw evidence.LoadRecords output, never applying the source:ci filter the closure fold applies (internal/evidence/candidates.go:16-27 via Fold/FoldFeature with Preview=false; closure-ergonomics co-1's authoritative-only posture, restated in ac-1's 'over authoritative evidence'). A derived tree containing a source:local passing record for a declared kind makes the detail layer read that kind as satisfied while the gate's ci-only fold reads the AC as unmet: the gate prints FAIL but unmetACDetail emits no line for exactly the kind whose authoritative evidence is missing (or no lines at all, so the 'missing-evidence detail' header never prints). This violates ac-1's 'Each unmet AC names its missing evidence kind and the exact on-disk path the fold reads for it' for that state, and undercuts dc-2's 'never by re-deriving eligibility through any independent path' — the per-kind satisfied check is re-derived over a different candidate set than the fold's, so the rendering can silently disagree with the verdict it annotates. Neither closepreflight_test.go nor closepreflightfeature_test.go constructs a SourceLocal record, so ac-3's per-defect-class agreement fixtures never exercise this divergence. (confidence 0.70)" }
  - { id: judged-feature-detail-applies-story-fold-kind-semantics, kind: judged, text: "For feature scope, runFeaturePreflightGate (cmd/verdi/closepreflightfeature.go:68-81) feeds unmet feature ACs into unmetACDetail, which treats every declared evidence kind as individually required (the STORY fold's conjunctive kindStatus rule, internal/evidence/fold.go:126-146). But the feature fold's own rule is different: foldFeatureAC (internal/evidence/featurefold.go:173-208) satisfies the outcome floor disjunctively (an authored attestation OR any current passing record) and an AC's pending status also folds in implementing-story closed/eligible state. Concretely: a feature AC declaring [behavioral, attestation] with an authored attestation, no behavioral record, and one still-open implementing story is pending solely because of the open story — yet preflight prints 'behavioral: no current passing record; derived-tree root probed: ...', naming as missing evidence a kind the shared fold does not read as required (the floor is already satisfied); symmetrically it prints the attestation scaffold/author remedy when a passing record already satisfies the floor. dc-4 binds disclosure granularity to 'exactly what the shared fold can see today, never invented further', and ac-1/outcome bind the detail to 'the exact artifact ... the fold reads' — a per-kind requirement the feature fold does not have is invented granularity that mis-directs the operator's remedy, even though no verdict drifts. (confidence 0.50)" }
  - { id: judged-violated-ac-with-coexisting-pass-record-unnamed, kind: judged, text: "A story AC that is violated while also holding a current passing record of the same declared kind (two producers: one pass, one fail — foldAC returns StatusViolated on any current fail, internal/evidence/fold.go:114-121) is in unmetACDetail's unmet set, but every declared kind reads as satisfied (a pass record exists, closepreflight.go:248-257), so no detail line prints. Because the story gate's own condition-1 rendering is the single static 'story is not eligible' line (closuregate.go:91), the story-scope preflight output then never names WHICH AC is violated or why — weaker than ac-1's per-unmet-AC naming intent and weaker than the feature gate's per-AC status list. ac-3's violated-class fixture (a lone fail record) passes because the pass-record coexistence case is what triggers the silence; it is plausible the spec's 'missing evidence kind' framing simply doesn't cover a violated-not-missing AC, hence low confidence. (confidence 0.25)" }
digest: sha256:a2d8b2eb4f5eb3fb642be06388ca6a787796f4f13496a877efc63bbbf8fa6ef2
integrity: sha256:6fed7e26597e48a3beeb8e90dbb0d4a3a50152b79c09f345477b401a17292acc
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"preflight-detail-skips-authoritative-source-filter\",\"text\":\"unmetACDetail (cmd/verdi/closepreflight.go:209,228) computes per-kind satisfaction over the raw evidence.LoadRecords output, never applying the source:ci filter the closure fold applies (internal/evidence/candidates.go:16-27 via Fold/FoldFeature with Preview=false; closure-ergonomics co-1's authoritative-only posture, restated in ac-1's 'over authoritative evidence'). A derived tree containing a source:local passing record for a declared kind makes the detail layer read that kind as satisfied while the gate's ci-only fold reads the AC as unmet: the gate prints FAIL but unmetACDetail emits no line for exactly the kind whose authoritative evidence is missing (or no lines at all, so the 'missing-evidence detail' header never prints). This violates ac-1's 'Each unmet AC names its missing evidence kind and the exact on-disk path the fold reads for it' for that state, and undercuts dc-2's 'never by re-deriving eligibility through any independent path' — the per-kind satisfied check is re-derived over a different candidate set than the fold's, so the rendering can silently disagree with the verdict it annotates. Neither closepreflight_test.go nor closepreflightfeature_test.go constructs a SourceLocal record, so ac-3's per-defect-class agreement fixtures never exercise this divergence.\",\"confidence\":0.7},{\"id\":\"feature-detail-applies-story-fold-kind-semantics\",\"text\":\"For feature scope, runFeaturePreflightGate (cmd/verdi/closepreflightfeature.go:68-81) feeds unmet feature ACs into unmetACDetail, which treats every declared evidence kind as individually required (the STORY fold's conjunctive kindStatus rule, internal/evidence/fold.go:126-146). But the feature fold's own rule is different: foldFeatureAC (internal/evidence/featurefold.go:173-208) satisfies the outcome floor disjunctively (an authored attestation OR any current passing record) and an AC's pending status also folds in implementing-story closed/eligible state. Concretely: a feature AC declaring [behavioral, attestation] with an authored attestation, no behavioral record, and one still-open implementing story is pending solely because of the open story — yet preflight prints 'behavioral: no current passing record; derived-tree root probed: ...', naming as missing evidence a kind the shared fold does not read as required (the floor is already satisfied); symmetrically it prints the attestation scaffold/author remedy when a passing record already satisfies the floor. dc-4 binds disclosure granularity to 'exactly what the shared fold can see today, never invented further', and ac-1/outcome bind the detail to 'the exact artifact ... the fold reads' — a per-kind requirement the feature fold does not have is invented granularity that mis-directs the operator's remedy, even though no verdict drifts.\",\"confidence\":0.5},{\"id\":\"violated-ac-with-coexisting-pass-record-unnamed\",\"text\":\"A story AC that is violated while also holding a current passing record of the same declared kind (two producers: one pass, one fail — foldAC returns StatusViolated on any current fail, internal/evidence/fold.go:114-121) is in unmetACDetail's unmet set, but every declared kind reads as satisfied (a pass record exists, closepreflight.go:248-257), so no detail line prints. Because the story gate's own condition-1 rendering is the single static 'story is not eligible' line (closuregate.go:91), the story-scope preflight output then never names WHICH AC is violated or why — weaker than ac-1's per-unmet-AC naming intent and weaker than the feature gate's per-AC status list. ac-3's violated-class fixture (a lone fail record) passes because the pass-record coexistence case is what triggers the silence; it is plausible the spec's 'missing evidence kind' framing simply doesn't cover a violated-not-missing AC, hence low confidence.\",\"confidence\":0.25}]}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/close-preflight@a032bcb2bd50824b90ac9f363946e058724fb9f9, spec/close-preflight@48d98b3a39de475e40b37255bc7ce257ccc21333], digest: sha256:a2d8b2eb4f5eb3fb642be06388ca6a787796f4f13496a877efc63bbbf8fa6ef2, integrity: sha256:6fed7e26597e48a3beeb8e90dbb0d4a3a50152b79c09f345477b401a17292acc }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-preflight-detail-skips-authoritative-source-filter** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: unmetACDetail (cmd/verdi/closepreflight.go:209,228) computes per-kind satisfaction over the raw evidence.LoadRecords output, never applying the source:ci filter the closure fold applies (internal/evidence/candidates.go:16-27 via Fold/FoldFeature with Preview=false; closure-ergonomics co-1's authoritative-only posture, restated in ac-1's 'over authoritative evidence'). A derived tree containing a source:local passing record for a declared kind makes the detail layer read that kind as satisfied while the gate's ci-only fold reads the AC as unmet: the gate prints FAIL but unmetACDetail emits no line for exactly the kind whose authoritative evidence is missing (or no lines at all, so the 'missing-evidence detail' header never prints). This violates ac-1's 'Each unmet AC names its missing evidence kind and the exact on-disk path the fold reads for it' for that state, and undercuts dc-2's 'never by re-deriving eligibility through any independent path' — the per-kind satisfied check is re-derived over a different candidate set than the fold's, so the rendering can silently disagree with the verdict it annotates. Neither closepreflight_test.go nor closepreflightfeature_test.go constructs a SourceLocal record, so ac-3's per-defect-class agreement fixtures never exercise this divergence. (confidence 0.70)
- **judged-feature-detail-applies-story-fold-kind-semantics** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: For feature scope, runFeaturePreflightGate (cmd/verdi/closepreflightfeature.go:68-81) feeds unmet feature ACs into unmetACDetail, which treats every declared evidence kind as individually required (the STORY fold's conjunctive kindStatus rule, internal/evidence/fold.go:126-146). But the feature fold's own rule is different: foldFeatureAC (internal/evidence/featurefold.go:173-208) satisfies the outcome floor disjunctively (an authored attestation OR any current passing record) and an AC's pending status also folds in implementing-story closed/eligible state. Concretely: a feature AC declaring [behavioral, attestation] with an authored attestation, no behavioral record, and one still-open implementing story is pending solely because of the open story — yet preflight prints 'behavioral: no current passing record; derived-tree root probed: ...', naming as missing evidence a kind the shared fold does not read as required (the floor is already satisfied); symmetrically it prints the attestation scaffold/author remedy when a passing record already satisfies the floor. dc-4 binds disclosure granularity to 'exactly what the shared fold can see today, never invented further', and ac-1/outcome bind the detail to 'the exact artifact ... the fold reads' — a per-kind requirement the feature fold does not have is invented granularity that mis-directs the operator's remedy, even though no verdict drifts. (confidence 0.50)
- **judged-violated-ac-with-coexisting-pass-record-unnamed** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: A story AC that is violated while also holding a current passing record of the same declared kind (two producers: one pass, one fail — foldAC returns StatusViolated on any current fail, internal/evidence/fold.go:114-121) is in unmetACDetail's unmet set, but every declared kind reads as satisfied (a pass record exists, closepreflight.go:248-257), so no detail line prints. Because the story gate's own condition-1 rendering is the single static 'story is not eligible' line (closuregate.go:91), the story-scope preflight output then never names WHICH AC is violated or why — weaker than ac-1's per-unmet-AC naming intent and weaker than the feature gate's per-AC status list. ac-3's violated-class fixture (a lone fail record) passes because the pass-record coexistence case is what triggers the silence; it is plausible the spec's 'missing evidence kind' framing simply doesn't cover a violated-not-missing AC, hence low confidence. (confidence 0.25)
